pre_registration.rationale: Attesting existing STABLE evidence from the 2026-04-21 robustness pass under the new leverage-card/v1 schema. Not an independent re-validation; the run artifacts and baseline parameters are unchanged.

objective.statement: Demonstrate that the SC-CONCEPT-0003 bidirectional-corridor fixed-point structure survives bounded parameter perturbation, with at most a small fraction of perturbations collapsing the corridor.
objective.metric_definition: collapsed_count / total_perturbations on a fixed 81-point parameter grid around the declared baseline

intervention.description: Re-run the SC-CONCEPT-0003 toy model over 20 bounded perturbations of the baseline parameters and classify each run by whether the bidirectional corridor survives.

denominators.compute: single-process Python; minutes wall time on the original validation machine
denominators.evidence_cost: low — deterministic CLI with committed result JSON

reversibility.rollback_method: git revert; pure offline computation, no persistent state beyond the research artifact directory

failure_conditions: collapsed_count > 0.1 * total_perturbations → corridor is not robust to bounded perturbation
collapsed_count is non-zero only at one perturbation axis → corridor depends on a hidden knob
structural_verdict diverges from collapsed_count classification → run-summary bookkeeping bug
Baseline parameters are silently retuned between runs → not a robustness pass

measured_outcome.summary: 0/20 perturbations collapsed the bidirectional corridor; structural_verdict STABLE. Concept SC-CONCEPT-0003 corridor is robust to bounded perturbation on the declared parameter grid.

decision.rationale: Retrospective attestation of T-012 under leverage-card/v1. Zero perturbations collapsed the corridor; verdict STABLE; matches the original 2026-04 result. Not an independent re-validation.
